name: ReactJs
scope: An individual at the ADVANCED proficiency level in React should have deep mastery of React fundamentals including functional and class components, JSX, props, state management, lifecycle methods, and React Hooks (useState, useEffect, useContext, useReducer), be proficient with advanced features like Context API, Refs, Error Boundaries, Portals, and understand React Fiber architecture and reconciliation process; they should have expertise in state management libraries such as Redux (including middleware like redux-thunk and redux-saga), MobX, or Context API, be able to design and implement higher-order components, render props, compound components, build reusable component libraries and design systems. They should be skilled in performance optimization techniques like code-splitting, lazy loading, memoization (React.memo, useMemo, useCallback), using performance profiling tools like React Profiler and browser DevTools; integrate TypeScript with React for type safety, defining complex types, interfaces, generics in components; experienced with testing frameworks like Jest, Enzyme, React Testing Library, able to write unit, integration, end-to-end tests, familiar with CI/CD pipelines for automated testing; proficient with build tools like Webpack, Babel, ESLint, and package managers like npm or Yarn. They should architect scalable and maintainable React applications, experience with micro-frontend architectures and modular design patterns; proficient in using Next.js for server-side rendering (SSR), understanding static site generation (SSG) and incremental static regeneration (ISR); skilled in consuming RESTful APIs and GraphQL endpoints, using Apollo Client or Relay; knowledge of implementing authentication mechanisms (JWT, OAuth), understanding security best practices to prevent XSS, CSRF, and other vulnerabilities. They should analyze and refactor existing codebases, upgrade React versions, migrate class components to functional components with hooks; set up monitoring tools like Sentry or New Relic for performance and error tracking; ensure applications comply with WCAG guidelines for accessibility, using tools like ARIA and screen readers; implement internationalization (i18n) and localization (l10n) using libraries like react-intl or i18next. They should lead code reviews, pair programming sessions, technical discussions; provide guidance and mentorship to junior and intermediate developers; familiar with project management tools like JIRA, Trello, Asana; write comprehensive technical documentation, API docs, user guides; keep up-to-date with latest React releases, RFCs, best practices; explore emerging technologies like React Native, React VR; participate in developer communities, conferences, open-source projects; share knowledge through blog posts, talks, workshops. They should debug complex issues using browser developer tools, React DevTools, profiling techniques; diagnose and fix memory leaks, race conditions, intricate bugs; ensure cross-browser and cross-platform compatibility; familiar with responsive design principles and CSS frameworks; possess effective communication skills, collaborating with UI/UX designers, backend developers, product managers; prioritize tasks effectively to meet project deadlines; balance multiple projects and responsibilities.

name: NextJs
scope: An individual at the ADVANCED proficiency level in Next.js should deeply understand advanced routing (dynamic routing, nested routes, custom route masking), master SSR, SSG, and ISR for optimal performance, and optimize applications using code splitting, lazy loading, caching strategies, and CDN integration. They should handle internationalization (i18n) with multilingual support and locale-based routing, integrate complex state management libraries like Redux or MobX, and efficiently manage state hydration between server and client. They implement secure authentication mechanisms (JWT, OAuth), address security vulnerabilities (XSS, CSRF), write comprehensive unit, integration, and end-to-end tests using tools like Jest and Cypress, and practice test-driven development (TDD). They design scalable architectures using microservices, serverless functions, and edge computing, integrate Next.js with backend services (including GraphQL APIs), handle complex data fetching patterns, and customize webpack and Babel configurations. They refactor and optimize existing codebases, deploy applications on platforms like Vercel or AWS, implement CI/CD pipelines, and set up monitoring tools like Sentry or New Relic for performance and error tracking. They possess advanced knowledge of React.js, experience with TypeScript, proficiency with UI libraries (Material-UI, Tailwind CSS, Styled Components), and manage dependencies efficiently using npm or Yarn workspaces. They lead technical discussions, review code, mentor junior and intermediate developers, conduct knowledge-sharing sessions, and drive technical excellence within the team. They deliver high-quality, robust, scalable, and high-performing Next.js applications that are accessible, SEO-friendly, and meet business requirements, while staying updated with the latest Next.js features and industry trends. They diagnose and fix complex bugs and performance issues, optimize applications for different environments, plan and execute migrations to Next.js, and develop long-term strategies for scalability and maintainability.
